homepage.heroSubtitle: An interactive tour from the modem squeal of the 90s to today's AI era. Open each period through its own model and authentic content.

heroPreview.twoTwentiesLabel: AI tools and a hyper-connected web.

slide90s.title: 90s
slide90s.label: First steps
slide90s.summary: The internet was slower, but every connection felt like a small adventure. The first websites, chats, and online communities were born.
highlights: First discussion forums and email lists
ICQ, GeoCities, and the Yahoo era

slide2000s.title: 2000s
slide2000s.label: Everyday use
slide2000s.summary: The web got faster with broadband and social networks. The internet moved from enthusiast circles into everyday life.
highlights: Blogs, forums, and early social networks
YouTube, Wikipedia, and early viral content

slide2010s.title: 2010s
slide2010s.label: Mobile revolution
slide2010s.summary: The mobile revolution and social media changed how we communicate and consume content. The internet became omnipresent.
highlights: Rise of smartphones and mobile internet
Boom of social networks and influencers

slidePresent.title: Present
slidePresent.label: AI and metaverse
slidePresent.summary: The internet is faster, smarter, and more connected than ever before. AI, the metaverse, and new technologies are shaping the future of the online world.
highlights: AI and machine learning in everyday life
Increased focus on privacy and security

web90s.underConstruction: 🚧 UNDER CONSTRUCTION 🚧 UNDER CONSTRUCTION 🚧 UNDER CONSTRUCTION 🚧
web90s.infoText: The 1990s were a turning point for technology. Personal computers began appearing in homes, the internet started connecting people across the world, and many devices we still use today began to take shape. Technologies were slower and less powerful than today, but they brought revolutionary possibilities - e-mail, web browsing, and digital communication. This exhibition takes you back to the era when the internet was just beginning to grow. Discover the devices and technologies that shaped the digital world of the 90s.

mouse.title: Computer Mouse
mouse.description: A 90s computer mouse was a basic tool for interacting with a computer. At that time, mice were often equipped with a ball that rotated while moving and transmitted movement data to the computer. These mice usually had two or three buttons, and sometimes a wheel for scrolling. They were essential for navigating graphical user interfaces and allowed users to control their computers more easily.

monitor.title: Monitor
monitor.description: Computer monitors in the 90s were large and heavy CRT screens (cathode-ray tubes). They looked similar to old televisions and could weigh more than 15 kilograms. They had lower resolution than modern displays and sometimes flickered slightly. Even so, they enabled colorful graphical interfaces, games, and the first web pages.

floppy.title: Floppy Disk
floppy.description: The floppy disk (most commonly 3.5-inch) was a common medium in the 90s for storing and transferring data. It had a capacity of 1.44 MB, enough for documents, small programs, or saved game files. It was also used for software installation and file transfer between computers. Although practical, it was sensitive to physical damage and magnetic fields.
